description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a classic Colonial architectural style, featuring a symmetrical facade and a steeply pitched gable roof covered in blue-gray shingles. The front of the house displays a combination of white siding and red brickwork, with black shutters accenting the rectangular windows. The main entryway is understated, with a single door flanked by windows and a small overhang above. A double garage with two doors is integrated into the front facade, and a prominent chimney rises from one side of the house. The windows are arranged in neat, symmetrical rows, contributing to the balanced appearance. The overall structure is boxy and solid, with clean lines and minimal decorative elements.
description: A two-story Colonial-style house with a blue-gray gable roof, white siding, red brick accents, black shutters, and a double garage. The facade is symmetrical with neatly arranged windows and a prominent chimney.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, double garage, red brickwork, and symmetrical window arrangement with black shutters.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the gable roof, chimney, and plain white siding with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: Rear or alternate side view with multiple windows, black shutters, and the chimney visible on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with plain white siding, gable roof, and no windows or doors visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Single front door with a small overhang, flanked by windows and brickwork, no porch or columns.
